mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent indicates deteriorating labor market conditions that suppress corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment stays above 5.0 for more than two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts reflect robust consumer spending and credit expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 5% in out-of-sample Sharpe ratio, or returns are negative when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that improves short-term profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon exclusively during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Predictive accuracy drops below 52% during expansion phases or the signal reverses direction when industrial production growth exceeds 3% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that supports household income and consumer spending, producing positive forward equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month window or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns in 60% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index surpassing 220.0 triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 65% of threshold-crossing months or the signal loses significance when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and accommodative federal funds rates creates a favorable macro environment that amplifies corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term explains less than 2% of return variance in out-of-sample testing or the combined signal underperforms independent proxies by 10% in Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production below 90.0 signals a manufacturing contraction that reduces business investment and consumer confidence, generating negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 60% of triggered instances or the threshold signal fails to maintain a Rank IC above 0.04 during contraction periods.
